analyses.name: Eli Lilly and Company
analyses.verdict: The GLP-1 franchise keeps compounding faster than the Street can model it -- TradingView's STRONG_BUY fully agrees.

financials.summary: Lilly's income statement is being carried by the Zepbound/Mounjaro (tirzepatide) obesity-and-diabetes franchise, which scaled to become one of the largest drug launches in industry history over the past two years. The scan shows EPS growth of roughly +170% YoY and revenue growth of roughly +56% YoY -- both by far the strongest fundamental prints of any name in this scan -- reflecting continued volume growth plus manufacturing capacity finally catching up with demand after years of supply constraints.
highlights: EPS growth ~+170% YoY
Revenue growth ~+56% YoY
Forward P/E ~27x -- a premium multiple, but backed by the fastest growth in large-cap pharma
Obesity/diabetes franchise (tirzepatide) is the primary growth driver

accomplishments: Scaled Zepbound/Mounjaro into one of the best-selling drug launches ever, aided by new manufacturing capacity coming online through 2025
Continued pipeline progress on oral GLP-1 candidates (orforglipron) aimed at expanding the obesity-drug market beyond injectables
Became one of the largest companies in the world by market cap on the back of the obesity-drug re-rating

journey_52w.summary: LLY's past year has been defined by resolving its own supply constraints -- once manufacturing caught up with tirzepatide demand, both volumes and margins expanded, and the stock has spent the year grinding toward new highs on a series of tight, orderly bases rather than one violent move.

milestones.label: Throughout the year
milestones.note: Sequential capacity expansion for tirzepatide manufacturing gradually resolved the supply bottleneck that had capped growth

milestones.label: Ongoing
milestones.note: Oral GLP-1 pipeline (orforglipron) progress kept the growth narrative alive beyond the injectable franchise

milestones.label: Current
milestones.note: RS line at new highs with a 5-contraction VCP tightened to 3.1% -- textbook continuation pattern

risks: Valuation leaves no room for a pipeline or manufacturing disappointment
Competitive GLP-1 dynamics (Novo Nordisk and emerging oral entrants) could pressure pricing over time
catalysts: Next earnings print Aug 5, 2026 -- watch tirzepatide volume/pricing trends
Oral GLP-1 (orforglipron) regulatory and pipeline updates
watch: RSI at 60 -- healthy, not yet overbought
Continued new 52-week highs would confirm trend strength
critical.valuation: 27x forward earnings is a premium to the market, but justified by ~56% revenue growth -- growth at a reasonable-relative-to-growth price rather than cheap.

analyses.name: Moderna, Inc.
analyses.verdict: A high-momentum bounce off a multi-year base -- TradingView's STRONG_BUY agrees, but RSI 86 means this is a name to manage tightly, not chase blindly.

financials.summary: Moderna's post-pandemic reset continues: COVID vaccine revenue has normalized to a much smaller, seasonal base, while the company leans on its broader respiratory-vaccine portfolio (COVID, RSV) and cost discipline to move back toward breakeven. The scan shows revenue growth of roughly +260% YoY off a very depressed prior-year comparison base -- a reflection of how low the trough was, not a sign the company is back to pandemic-era scale.
highlights: Revenue growth ~+260% YoY off a depressed prior-year base
Forward P/E is negative (still working toward sustained profitability)
RSV and next-gen respiratory vaccine portfolio broadening beyond COVID

accomplishments: Continued build-out of its respiratory-vaccine portfolio (COVID + RSV combination approaches) beyond the single-product COVID franchise
Ongoing cost-reduction program aimed at reaching sustained profitability post-pandemic
Received renewed sell-side attention (including a widely-noted shift from a long-time skeptic) as cost cuts and pipeline diversification progress

journey_52w.summary: MRNA spent much of the past two years in a steep post-pandemic drawdown as COVID vaccine demand normalized far below the 2021-2022 peak. The past year has seen the stock stabilize and begin repairing off that multi-year low as cost cuts took hold and the market started pricing in a smaller but more durable respiratory-vaccine business.

milestones.label: Past 12 months
milestones.note: Stock based near multi-year lows before beginning a repair as cost discipline showed up in results

milestones.label: Recent
milestones.note: Positive sell-side commentary shift (including a notable bullish turn from a longtime market commentator) added to sentiment tailwinds

milestones.label: Current
milestones.note: RS line at new highs with a 5-contraction VCP -- a genuine trend change, not just a dead-cat bounce

risks: RSI 86 -- extremely overbought; a sharp pullback is a real near-term risk after this size of a move
Business is still smaller and less predictable than the pandemic-era run rate
Forward P/E still negative -- profitability isn't fully proven yet
catalysts: Earnings Jul 31, 2026 -- watch for confirmation that cost cuts are sustainable
watch: Given RSI 86, this is a trim-into-strength name, not an add-here name
Watch for the RS trend to hold through any near-term cooling
critical.valuation: Negative forward P/E reflects a company still working back to sustained profitability -- this is a turnaround/momentum trade, not a value one.

analyses.name: The Bank of New York Mellon
analyses.verdict: The highest-quality technical setup in the entire scan, and the fundamentals back it up -- but no independent TradingView read exists to confirm it.

financials.summary: As the world's largest custody bank, BNY Mellon has benefited from higher-for-longer rates on client cash balances plus continued growth in its securities-services and asset-servicing fee businesses, including a push into digital-asset custody. The scan shows EPS growth of roughly +42% YoY and revenue growth of roughly +13% YoY, a strong combination of margin expansion and top-line growth for a mega-cap bank.
highlights: EPS growth ~+42% YoY
Revenue growth ~+13% YoY
Forward P/E ~14x -- inexpensive relative to its growth rate
Expanding into digital-asset custody services alongside its core securities-servicing franchise

accomplishments: Continued expansion of digital-asset custody and tokenization services for institutional clients
Sustained fee growth across securities services and asset servicing even as market volatility normalized
Efficiency initiatives drove EPS growth well ahead of revenue growth

journey_52w.summary: BK has spent the past year in a steady, low-volatility uptrend typical of the money-center/custody-bank group, aided by resilient net interest income and fee growth, culminating in a tight coiling pattern right below its 52-week high heading into earnings season.

milestones.label: Through the year
milestones.note: Steady fee and NII growth kept the stock in a persistent uptrend with shallow pullbacks

milestones.label: Recent quarters
milestones.note: Digital-asset custody build-out added a new growth narrative on top of the core custody-bank business

milestones.label: Current
milestones.note: 2-contraction VCP tightened to just 3.0%, RS at new highs, 2.5% off the 52-week high heading into the Jul 15 print

risks: Earnings Jul 15, 2026 is only 12 days out -- binary event risk into the print
No independent TradingView rating resolved for this ticker -- this call rests entirely on the scan's own data
catalysts: Jul 15 earnings -- watch net interest income and fee growth trends
watch: A break of the $147.29 52-week high on volume would confirm the breakout
Consider trimming into strength ahead of the earnings print given the binary risk
critical.valuation: 14x forward earnings against 42% EPS growth is a genuinely cheap multiple for the growth rate -- one of the better risk/reward setups in the scan on valuation grounds alone.

analyses.name: American Electric Power
analyses.verdict: The cleanest expression of the AI-power-demand utility trade -- TradingView's STRONG_BUY with zero sell votes fully confirms.

financials.summary: AEP, one of the largest regulated electric utilities in the US, is benefiting from surging electricity demand tied to data-center and industrial reshoring growth across its service territories (including a heavy presence in the mid-Atlantic/Ohio Valley data-center corridor). The scan shows steady EPS growth of roughly +7% YoY and revenue growth of roughly +10% YoY -- modest by growth-stock standards but strong for a regulated utility, reflecting rate-base growth tied to that demand.
highlights: EPS growth ~+7% YoY
Revenue growth ~+10% YoY
Forward P/E ~20x -- a premium utility multiple reflecting the data-center growth story
Large exposure to data-center-driven load growth in its transmission territory

accomplishments: Continued capital investment in transmission infrastructure to serve growing data-center and industrial demand
Raised or reaffirmed longer-term earnings growth guidance as electricity demand forecasts across its territory were revised upward
Maintained regulated-utility credit quality while funding an elevated capex program

journey_52w.summary: AEP's past year tracks the broader re-rating of power utilities as AI/data-center electricity demand forecasts were repeatedly revised higher, turning a historically slow-growth regulated utility into one of the market's more interesting secular-growth stories, with the stock grinding steadily toward new highs.

milestones.label: Through the year
milestones.note: Data-center demand forecasts for AEP's territory were revised upward multiple times, supporting a re-rating of the growth outlook

milestones.label: Current
milestones.note: 3-contraction VCP tightened to 2.6%, essentially at the 52-week high

risks: RSI 73 -- stretched short-term; a pullback to the 50-day would be a healthier entry than chasing here
Rate-sensitive as a utility -- a hawkish Fed surprise would pressure the whole group
catalysts: Earnings Jul 30, 2026 -- watch for updated data-center demand and capex guidance
watch: A breakout above the 52-week high on volume confirms continuation
Watch for the broader utility-sector rotation (AEP/DUK/DTE) to broaden or stall
critical.valuation: 20x forward earnings is a premium for a regulated utility, priced for the data-center-driven rate-base growth to continue.

analyses.name: Terreno Realty
analyses.verdict: A high-quality small-cap industrial REIT with the strongest TV consensus of any REIT in this scan.

financials.summary: Terreno owns and operates industrial/logistics properties in six major coastal US markets. The scan shows EPS growth of roughly +40% YoY and revenue growth of roughly +13% YoY, reflecting continued strong releasing spreads (re-leasing expiring space at meaningfully higher rents) as e-commerce and logistics demand keeps industrial vacancy low in its target markets.
highlights: EPS growth ~+40% YoY
Revenue growth ~+13% YoY
Forward P/E ~38.7x -- priced for continued execution
Concentrated in six high-barrier-to-entry coastal industrial markets

accomplishments: Continued to post strong cash releasing spreads as expiring industrial leases were renewed at significantly higher market rents
Maintained a disciplined acquisition strategy focused on infill, high-barrier coastal industrial markets rather than chasing cheaper secondary markets

journey_52w.summary: TRNO's past year reflects the broader resilience of coastal infill industrial real estate -- even as some secondary-market industrial REITs saw softening, Terreno's supply-constrained coastal footprint kept occupancy and rent growth strong, and the stock has spent the year building a tight base right at its highs.

milestones.label: Through the year
milestones.note: Consistent double-digit cash releasing spreads reported each quarter as leases rolled over

milestones.label: Current
milestones.note: 4-contraction VCP tightened to 5.1%, sitting right at the 52-week high

risks: Small-cap REIT -- thinner liquidity than the mega-cap names on this pick list
Fwd P/E 38.7x is rich; a slowdown in releasing spreads would pressure the multiple
Earnings Aug 6, 2026
catalysts: Aug 6 earnings -- watch same-store NOI growth and releasing spreads
watch: A clean breakout above the 52-week high vs. a false start at resistance
critical.valuation: 38.7x forward earnings is expensive even by REIT standards -- this is a quality premium, not a value entry.

analyses.name: Fortinet, Inc.
analyses.verdict: A held position -- cybersecurity demand keeps compounding, but TradingView's plain BUY (not STRONG_BUY) is a touch softer than our score implies.

financials.summary: Fortinet continues to benefit from enterprise consolidation onto its SASE (secure access service edge) and network-security platform as global cybersecurity spending keeps growing double digits. The scan shows EPS growth of roughly +29% YoY and revenue growth of roughly +20% YoY, reflecting continued share gains as customers consolidate point-security-tools spend onto fewer, broader platforms.
highlights: EPS growth ~+29% YoY
Revenue growth ~+20% YoY
Forward P/E ~46x -- the richest multiple in this pick list
SASE/platform-consolidation trend continues to drive share gains

accomplishments: Continued platform consolidation wins as enterprises reduce their number of point-security vendors in favor of Fortinet's integrated SASE offering
Benefited from broader industry tailwinds as global cybersecurity spend was recently estimated to have crossed the $300B mark

journey_52w.summary: FTNT has spent the past year as one of the more consistent performers in enterprise security, riding the SASE-consolidation trend to a series of higher highs and tight bases, even as some higher-multiple software peers saw more volatility.

milestones.label: Through the year
milestones.note: Consistent SASE/platform-consolidation bookings growth kept the stock in a persistent uptrend

milestones.label: Current
milestones.note: RS line at new highs, 4-contraction VCP tightened to 3.6%, only 1.7% off the 52-week high

risks: TradingView's plain BUY (14/5/7) is softer than our A-grade score implies -- 5 of 20 indicators lean sell/neutral, worth noting the disagreement
Fwd P/E 46x is the richest multiple on this pick list -- no room for a growth deceleration
catalysts: Earnings Jul 30, 2026 -- watch billings growth as the read on demand durability
watch: Confirm the TV disagreement resolves toward STRONG_BUY, or watch billings growth closely at the print
critical.valuation: 46x forward earnings prices in continued 20%+ revenue growth -- expensive, but the growth has been consistently delivered.

analyses.name: SiriusXM Holdings
analyses.verdict: A held position -- RS at new highs and a unanimous TradingView STRONG_BUY, but no clean VCP base means this is momentum, not a textbook breakout.

financials.summary: SiriusXM's satellite-radio and streaming subscription business has stabilized after a multi-year period of slow subscriber growth, helped by bundling initiatives and cost discipline. The scan shows EPS growth of roughly +22% YoY on revenue growth of roughly +1% YoY -- a story of margin expansion and buybacks driving EPS well ahead of a roughly flat top line.
highlights: EPS growth ~+22% YoY on roughly flat (+1%) revenue
Forward P/E ~9x -- one of the cheapest multiples in this pick list
Berkshire Hathaway remains a large, well-known holder of the stock

accomplishments: Continued cost discipline and buyback activity drove EPS growth well ahead of a roughly flat top line
Bundling and streaming-platform initiatives aimed at stabilizing subscriber trends after years of slow declines/flat growth

journey_52w.summary: SIRI has been a value/turnaround story for much of the past few years given slow subscriber growth, but the past year has seen renewed momentum as cost discipline and buybacks pushed EPS higher even without top-line acceleration, culminating in a move to new relative-strength highs.

milestones.label: Through the year
milestones.note: Buyback-driven EPS growth continued even as subscriber growth stayed roughly flat

milestones.label: Current
milestones.note: RS line at new highs; no clear VCP base has formed, so this reads as a momentum move rather than a coiled-spring breakout

risks: No clear VCP base -- give this wider stops than the textbook-base names on this list, since there's no obvious support level
RSI 87 -- very extended, a sharp reversal is a real risk here
Revenue growth is nearly flat (+1%) -- EPS growth is coming from buybacks/margin, not the top line
catalysts: Any acceleration in subscriber or streaming-bundle metrics
watch: Given RSI 87, trim into strength rather than add here
critical.valuation: 9x forward earnings is cheap, but reflects the market's skepticism about the flat top line -- this is a value-with-momentum story, not a growth one.

analyses.name: Applied Materials
analyses.verdict: A held position -- strong fundamentals, but the chart is still 16.6% below its 52-week high, meaning this is a repair story, not a fresh breakout.

financials.summary: As one of the largest semiconductor-equipment makers, Applied Materials benefits from the ongoing capex build-out across leading-edge logic and memory fabs. The scan shows EPS growth of roughly +34% YoY and revenue growth of roughly +11% YoY, reflecting continued strong demand for wafer-fab-equipment tied to AI-driven chip capacity expansion, even as the stock's price action has lagged the fundamentals.
highlights: EPS growth ~+34% YoY
Revenue growth ~+11% YoY
Forward P/E ~37x
Still 16.6% below its 52-week high despite the strong fundamental print

accomplishments: Continued to benefit from elevated fab-equipment capex tied to AI/leading-edge chip capacity expansion across foundry and memory customers
Maintained EPS growth well ahead of revenue growth through operating leverage

journey_52w.summary: AMAT's past year has been more volatile than the broader semiconductor-equipment recovery story would suggest, with the stock pulling back meaningfully from its highs even as fundamentals stayed strong -- a disconnect that makes this a name to watch for a technical confirmation rather than assume the fundamentals alone are enough.

milestones.label: Through the year
milestones.note: Fundamentals stayed strong through the AI capex cycle even as the stock pulled back from highs on cyclical/export-control concerns common across the semi-equipment group

milestones.label: Current
milestones.note: 5-contraction VCP, 2.1% tightest contraction, but still 16.6% below the 52-week high

risks: 16.6% below the 52-week high -- still in repair mode, not a confirmed breakout
Semiconductor-equipment names remain sensitive to export-control and China-demand headlines
Earnings Aug 14, 2026 -- still six weeks out
catalysts: A reclaim of the 50-day moving average and a fresh higher high would upgrade this from WATCH to a live setup
watch: Don't chase here -- wait for confirmation of a base completion before adding meaningfully
critical.valuation: 37x forward earnings against 34% EPS growth is reasonable for the growth rate, but the stock needs to prove the base is complete before that valuation gets fully credited.

analyses.name: Simon Property Group
analyses.verdict: A held position -- best-in-class mall REIT with RS at new highs and a unanimous-leaning TradingView STRONG_BUY.

financials.summary: Simon, the largest mall/premium-outlet REIT in the US, continues to post strong results as premium retail traffic and occupancy at its top-tier properties remain resilient even amid a mixed broader retail backdrop. The scan shows EPS growth of roughly +16% YoY and revenue growth of roughly +19% YoY, among the strongest top-line growth of any REIT in this scan.
highlights: EPS growth ~+16% YoY
Revenue growth ~+19% YoY -- the strongest top-line growth of any REIT in this pick list
Forward P/E ~33.5x

accomplishments: Maintained high occupancy and strong releasing spreads across its flagship mall and premium-outlet portfolio
Continued to outperform lower-quality mall peers as retail traffic concentrated further into top-tier, well-located properties

journey_52w.summary: SPG's past year reflects the broader 'flight to quality' in retail real estate -- as weaker regional malls continued to struggle, premium properties like Simon's kept posting strong traffic and occupancy, and the stock has climbed steadily to new relative-strength highs.

milestones.label: Through the year
milestones.note: Consistent occupancy and releasing-spread strength at flagship properties despite a mixed broader retail landscape

milestones.label: Current
milestones.note: RS line at new highs, 4-contraction VCP tightened to 4.2%, just 0.7% off the 52-week high

risks: Fwd P/E 33.5x prices in continued strength
Earnings Aug 11, 2026 -- the furthest-out catalyst of the REIT names in this scan
catalysts: Aug 11 earnings -- watch occupancy and releasing-spread trends
watch: A shallow, 0.7%-off-high pullback -- watch for the retest of the highs to hold as support
critical.valuation: 33.5x forward earnings is a premium REIT multiple, justified by best-in-class revenue growth (+19% YoY) relative to REIT peers.
